title: Vertex AI ML Pipeline Automation
summary: End-to-end automated ML training, deployment, and monitoring pipelines on Google Cloud.

content.value: AI/ML workloads at TouchNet required repeatable, production-grade pipelines that could handle training, evaluation, and deployment without manual intervention. The existing process relied on ad-hoc scripts that broke frequently under high-load scenarios.

content.title: Key Design Decision
content.value: We chose Kubeflow Pipelines over Vertex AI Managed Pipelines to retain portability — the same DAG compiles to run on any Kubeflow cluster, decoupling us from GCP vendor lock-in at the pipeline layer.

Designed and deployed automated pipelines on Google Cloud Vertex AI and Kubeflow. Each pipeline stage — data ingestion, preprocessing, training, evaluation, and model registration — was containerized and orchestrated via Kubeflow Pipelines DSL. Terraform managed all GCP infrastructure including Vertex AI endpoints, GCS buckets, and IAM bindings.

content.label: pipeline.py

content.title: Gotcha
content.value: Vertex AI Pipelines serialises component arguments as JSON. Avoid passing large numpy arrays between components — always write to GCS and pass the URI instead, or pipeline submission latency spikes dramatically.

Integrated pipeline triggers with GitHub Actions so any merge to main automatically kicked off a training run. Monitoring dashboards tracked model drift, latency, and error budgets via SLIs. Failed runs triggered PagerDuty alerts and auto-rollback to the last stable model version.

content.title: Outcome
content.value: Reduced manual deployment effort by 70%, achieved 99.9% pipeline uptime over 12 months, and enabled the data science team to ship model updates 3× faster than before.
